description_long: This object is a vintage television with a rectangular wooden exterior and a large, convex glass screen that dominates the front face. The screen is bordered by a thick, rounded bezel, and to the right of the screen are two prominent rotary dials and a small rectangular button or switch, all set into a black panel. The sides and top are finished in a dark, polished wood grain, while the back features a protruding, boxy metal housing with visible ventilation slats and screws. The left side has two circular speaker grilles, suggesting built-in audio output. The overall design is bulky and retro, typical of mid-20th-century analog televisions, and the object appears solid and heavy, likely containing internal components such as cathode ray tubes and wiring.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a wood-grain exterior, large convex glass screen, and rotary dials on a black side panel. The back is a protruding metal housing, and the left side features two speaker grilles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large convex glass screen, thick bezel, and two rotary dials with a button on the right black panel.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the wood grain finish and two circular speaker grilles, with the metal back housing extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the protruding black metal housing with ventilation slats and screws, and the wooden top and sides.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the uninterrupted wood grain panel and the depth of the metal back housing.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, rounded, dark brown wood bezel with a polished finish and retro styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of two rotary dials and one button on the front right black panel.
